perturbation.original_text: 4. Each part y shall hold in trust and confi dence and prot ect and treat as strictly secret and confidential at all times the Inform ation received from the other party, and in respect of su ch Inform ation, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, m anipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordan ce with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose; NDA Page 2 of 2 (e) shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take the sam e precautions to protect the Inform ation as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation and, i n doing so, shal l use reasonabl e care t o protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored in a tangible form segregat ed from all of its own propert y and i n a safe and secure envi ronm ent and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever (g) shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y, and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be; (i) shall treat co mmunicatio ns with the other party as co nfidential an d utilise reaso nable care in maintaining the confidentiality o f communicatio ns even if th ey do not include Information: and (j) shall be liable to the other party fo r compliance with the term s and conditions of this Ag reem ent, and shall fully indem nify the other part y and hol d it harmless from dam ages (i ncluding lawyers’ fees and disbursem ents) incurred as a result of a breach of this Agreem ent.

perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the original agreement by now allowing for disclosure of information with prior written consent. This conflicts with the original clause 4(a) that strictly prohibits any direct or indirect disclosure of information, creating an in-text contradiction within clause 4 about whether disclosure is permissible, even with consent. This introduces ambiguity that can lead to legal disputes over the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.justification: The original text of clause 4(a) strictly prohibits any direct or indirect disclosure of information. The changed text modifies this clause to allow disclosure with prior written consent. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition, creating an in-text contradiction within clause 4 itself. The contradiction is that clause 4(a) both prohibits and allows disclosure of information, depending on whether written consent is given. This creates a strong, clear inconsistency in the contract. This adheres to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, including m onetary dam ages, t o obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties agree that monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any violation of this agreement. The parties hereby waive their right to seek injunctive relief or any other equitable remedies.
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts the original agreement by stating monetary damages as the sole remedy. This directly conflicts with the original clause 8, which allows for injunctive relief and mandatory court orders, creating an in-text contradiction. The newly inserted waiver creates uncertainty as to which remedies are actually available in case of a violation of the NDA obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties agree that monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any violation of this agreement. The parties hereby waive their right to seek injunctive relief or any other equitable remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that monetary damages are the sole and exclusive remedy, and waives the right to injunctive relief, which directly contradicts the original clause 8 that allows for injunctive relief and other equitable remedies. The changed text and the contradicted text are the same, showing that the contradictory language was introduced in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: 14. This Agreem ent shall inure t o the benefi t of and be bi nding upon t he part ies heret o and t heir respect ive successo rs an d permitted assig ns. Neith er party sh all assig n, transfer o r otherwise alien ate its rig hts and obligations hereunder in whole or in part, without the prior writte n consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. This Agreem ent shall inure t o the benefi t of and be bi nding upon t he part ies heret o and t heir respect ive successo rs an d permitted assig ns. Either party may assig n, transfer o r otherwise alien ate its rig hts and obligations hereunder in whole or in part, with or without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The change allows either party to assign rights and obligations without consent, directly contradicting the original clause 14, which requires prior written consent. This creates a direct contradiction within the clause itself, raising uncertainty about assignment conditions and potentially leading to disputes over who holds contractual rights and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. This Agreem ent shall inure t o the benefi t of and be bi nding upon t he part ies heret o and t heir respect ive successo rs an d permitted assig ns. Either party may assig n, transfer o r otherwise alien ate its rig hts and obligations hereunder in whole or in part, with or without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 14 to allow either party to assign rights and obligations without the other party's consent. The contradicted text, which is clause 14 itself, now states conflicting terms within the same clause. The original text requires prior written consent, while the changed text allows assignment with or without consent. This is a direct inconsistency as it creates a contradiction within the clause, leading to uncertainty regarding the assignment conditions.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The parties acknowledge that no representation or warranty has been m ade as to the accuracy or completeness of any Inform ation whi ch may be suppl ied and it is ag reed that neither party sh all have any liability, d irect o r indirect, to the other as a resu lt of the use of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The parties acknowledge that no representation or warranty has been m ade as to the accuracy or completeness of any Inform ation whi ch may be suppl ied and it is ag reed that both party shall have liability, d irect o r indirect, to the other as a resu lt of the use of the Information. The extent of this liability is capped at £100,000.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original agreement by imposing liability on both parties for the use of information, whereas the original stated neither party shall have any liability. The introduction of liability with a cap creates an in-text contradiction about whether any liability exists, creating uncertainty and disputes on how the parties will deal with this liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The parties acknowledge that no representation or warranty has been m ade as to the accuracy or completeness of any Inform ation whi ch may be suppl ied and it is ag reed that both party shall have liability, d irect o r indirect, to the other as a resu lt of the use of the Information. The extent of this liability is capped at £100,000.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party shall have any liability. The changed text states that both parties shall have liability, capped at £100,000. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Therefore, the change introduces a direct contradiction to the original agreement regarding liability, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
